assertion.text: Erdős Problem #204 has status 'disproved (lean)'. Statement: Are there $n$ such that there is a covering system with moduli the divisors of $n$ which is 'as disjoint as possible'? That is, for all $d\mid n$ with $d>1$ there is an associated $a_d$ such that every integer is congruent to some $a_d\pmod{d}$, and if there is some integer $x$ with $$x\equiv a_d\pmod{d}\textrm{ and }x\equiv a_{d'}\pmod{d'}$$then $(d,d')=1$. The density of such $n$ is zero. Erdős and Graham believed that no such $n$ exist. Adenwalla [Ad25] has proved there are no such $n$. This was formalized by van Doorn in Lean using Aristotle. Topics: covering systems. Erdős prize: no. Statement is machine-verified in Lean (formal-conjectures). OEIS: N/A.
